mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and causing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, invalidating the labor deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad demand collapse, leading to negative forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a win rate above 40 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term equity returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases precede negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases during early-cycle expansions, breaking the momentum link.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20d returns, indicating markets discount tightening fears.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yield negative 20d returns in >50% of observations, negating the earnings boost channel.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side contraction, reducing profit margins and causing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing below its 12-month average precedes positive 20d returns in >65% of cases, invalidating the contraction signal.
